parent.label: Synthetic Data
parent.description: Text generated by AI models to train other (or future versions of the same) AI models. Synthetic data is used primarily for pre-training augmentation in areas where human-generated data is scarce — especially formal mathematics and structured reasoning. Its use in pretraining is recent (2023+) and growing, though it constitutes a small share of current corpora.
bullets: rStar-Math: 747K math problems with step-by-step reasoning, generated by LLMs for LLM training
Phi-1, Phi-2, Phi-3 (Microsoft): deliberately trained on AI-generated 'textbook quality' text
Risk of 'model collapse': models trained on synthetic data derived from themselves may degrade over generations

cells.label: Synthetic Reasoning
cells.description: AI-generated reasoning traces and solutions for mathematics, logic, and coding — designed to teach models the step-by-step decomposition of problems. This is the fastest-growing new category in pre-training data.
bullets: rStar-Math (Microsoft): 747K math competition problems with Monte Carlo tree search-generated solutions
NuminaMath, DART-Math: augmented math datasets for competition-level problem solving
Recursive self-improvement: a model generates training data that trains a better version of itself
cells.screenshotCaption: rStar-Math on arXiv — AI-generated mathematical reasoning traces used for training
